title: sempervivum tectorum var. corymbosum
intro: Sempervivum tectorum var. corymbosum, commonly known as the houseleek or corymbous houseleek, is a succulent plant that belongs to the Crassulaceae family. Traditionally, the plant has been used for its medicinal properties, including antiseptic and anti-inflammatory effects, which have been used to treat wounds, skin conditions, and other ailments. The plant has been reported to have various health benefits, such as reducing inflammation, promoting wound healing, and acting as an antimicrobial agent. In herbal preparations, Sempervivum tectorum var. corymbosum is often used as a tincture or salve to treat skin conditions, and is also used in traditional medicine to treat digestive issues and other health problems.
